llm.summary: Code and verification are strong, but the repository does not yet present one unambiguous public-publishing contract repo-wide. The mixed notes-plus-links behavior is implemented, yet key docs still describe a notes-only public homepage, so exit criterion 1 is not satisfied in substance.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Exit criterion 2 is substantively met: links now have explicit publish state, remain private by default, and unpublished links are excluded from public routes in code and E2E coverage.
Exit criterion 3 is met: the provided deterministic check shows `npm run verify` passing.
missing_requirements: Exit criterion 1 is still incomplete repo-wide: `docs/design-docs/minakeep-information-architecture.md` says `/` is for published notes only, and `docs/product-specs/public-note-publishing.md` still says the homepage lists only published notes. Those contradictions leave the notes-plus-links publishing boundary ambiguous.

The active queue is now out of sync with shipped behavior: `docs/exec-plans/active/index.md` and queued tasks `032-owner-link-publish-controls.md` and `033-public-mixed-showroom-feed.md` still treat already-implemented behavior as future work. That weakens the clarity required for promotion.
human_review_triggers: Publishing boundaries for notes versus links are still ambiguous because repo docs disagree about whether `/` is notes-only or mixed public content.
The implementation appears to have absorbed work from queued tasks 032 and 033; the active Ralph queue likely needs reconciliation before promotion.

summary: Code and verification are strong, but the repository does not yet present one unambiguous public-publishing contract repo-wide. The mixed notes-plus-links behavior is implemented, yet key docs still describe a notes-only public homepage, so exit criterion 1 is not satisfied in substance.
